title: Matching crop row and dripline distance in subsurface drip irrigation increases yield and mitigates N2O emissions
description: A two-year field study evaluates how subsurface drip irrigation (SDI) with different dripline spacings affects maize and soybean yield, nitrogen use efficiency, and nitrous oxide emissions under no-till conservation agriculture. The research compares narrow (70 cm) and wide (140 cm) SDI layouts against sprinkler irrigation in a fine-textured soil in Northern Italy to identify optimal management for productivity and greenhouse gas mitigation.
